commit.message:
Dramatic scroll-driven animations: scale, blur, fade tied to scroll position

Major changes:
- CSS animation-timeline: view() for Chrome/Edge/Safari (85%+ users)
  Sections scale from 60% to 100% as they enter viewport center
  Blur 8px at edges, sharp at center. Proper fade out on exit.
- JS uses document directly (st.html is NOT iframed in 1.55+)
- Fix: window.parent.document → document
- Fix: marker spans now non-empty ( ) to survive DOMPurify
- Fix: MutationObserver debounced with setTimeout to prevent thrash
- Edge vignette: dark gradient overlays at top/bottom of viewport
- Fallback: Firefox gets simple one-shot fade+rise via IntersectionObserver
- Child stagger via scroll-child keyframes with tighter animation ranges
- prefers-reduced-motion: all animations disabled

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add experiments/theoretical_calculations: 5 first-principles experiments

All experiments run from hardware spec numbers only — no simulator,
no GPU, no external data. Pure statistical mechanics.

01_carnot_curve.py
  H100 β-sweep: η_hw(β), S(β), Cv(β), ln Z decomposition.
  Result: η_hw,max = 96.30% at β_optimal = 10.0; roofline ridge
  = 0.51 FLOP/byte recovered exactly from the Carnot AI_min condition.

02_memory_hierarchy.py
  Transfer matrix thermal fingerprint: effective temperatures
  (reg=1×, smem=23×, L2=200×, HBM=600×), spectral norms per level,
  minimum reuse factors, roofline ridge vs HBM bandwidth sweep.

03_scaling_efficiency.py
  η_multi,max vs n_gpu (1→64) for NVLink / NVSwitch / PCIe / IB.
  Key finding: IB η_overlap degrades to 0.80 at 64 GPUs vs 0.98
  for NVLink; PCIe ring holds scaling best due to O(N) edge count.

04_resonance.py
  η_overlap 2D heatmap over (T_compute, T_comm) space.
  LLaMA-7B on 8 GPUs is severely compute-dominated (T_c ≈ 22s
  vs T_k ≤ 2.5s); all configs sit far below the resonance ridge.

05_parallelism_optimizer.py
  Full Pareto frontiers for GPT-2 Small/8-GPU and LLaMA-7B/64-GPU.
  GPT-2: all 9 configs Pareto-efficient, TP2+PP4 recommended.
  LLaMA-7B: DP16+TP4 recommended (η_multi=0.81, η_overlap=0.84).

Also adds matplotlib>=3.9 as optional [experiments] dependency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: show block/node count in subtitle for debugging

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Giles Cope <gilescope@gmail.com>

[BugFix] Fix ROCm/HIP kernel launch using CUDA-only API (#1905)

* [BugFix] Fix ROCm/HIP kernel launch using CUDA-only cudaLaunchKernelEx API

Commit 2e854b06 introduced cluster launch support by refactoring the kernel
dispatch in `create_dispatch_func` to use `KERNEL_LAUNCH_FUNC_CODE`, which
calls `cudaLaunchKernelEx` — a CUDA-only API not available in HIP/ROCm.
`TLHIPSourceWrapper` inherits this method and thus generates invalid code
for HIP targets, breaking the ROCm CI.

Fix by:
- Adding `KERNEL_LAUNCH_FUNC_CODE_HIP` using the `<<<>>>` syntax supported by hipcc
- Extracting kernel launch code selection into a `get_kernel_launch_code` method
- Overriding `get_kernel_launch_code` in `TLHIPSourceWrapper` to use the HIP template

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* lint

Fix search bar autofill: switch to type=text with autocomplete=nope

Chrome ignores autocomplete="off" near auth forms and tries to fill
search bars with saved credentials. Using type="text" + autocomplete="nope"
(an unrecognized value) prevents the browser from triggering password/email autofill.

fix: confirm_table_payment_v05 fails after card capture infrastructure removal

Migration 20260508000007 dropped the card_captures table and
card_capture_required column, but confirm_table_payment_v05 still
referenced both — causing every Stripe webhook deposit confirmation
to fail with a DB error. Rewrites the function to remove those
references while preserving all other payment confirmation logic.

commit.message:
Add F/C distractor tiles, fix label centering, add Solfege mode to Pentatonic Trainer

- Added F and C distractor tiles to the note palette, visible only in Treble Clef Mode. These are non-solfege notes that can be placed on any note position but are always marked incorrect, helping students distinguish which note names apply to this scale.
- Fixed note label centering: switched from offsetWidth-based calculation to CSS transform: translate(-50%, -50%) on .draggable-label.placed, which correctly centers labels regardless of text width or layout timing.
- In Treble Clef Mode, the "Note Names" settings button changes to "Solfege" and instead displays solfege syllables above the note circles on the staff (rather than concert pitch letters, which are already the label tiles being dragged).

commit.message:
feat: add webhook agent loop reference implementation (#239)

* feat: add webhook agent loop reference implementation

Adds a standalone agent-loop/ directory with a webhook-driven agent that
closes the human-AI feedback loop. When a reviewer leaves a comment,
the agent receives the webhook, calls Claude, posts a reply, and
resolves the comment automatically.

Includes:
- HTTP server with HMAC-SHA256 signature verification
- Remarq API client with configurable timeouts
- Prompt builder for comment context formatting
- Agent orchestration with self-loop, reply, and idempotency guards
- 24 tests, 83%+ line coverage
- ADR-001 documenting the directory pattern decision
- README, CHANGELOG, and best-practices cross-references

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address Council review feedback on agent-loop PR

- Fix fire-and-forget bug: use .catch() instead of try/catch on async
- Use crypto.timingSafeEqual() for HMAC signature comparison
- Add request body size limit (1MB) to readBody
- Handle premature request close in readBody
- Add reply guard debug log for consistency
- Expose CLAUDE_MODEL as env var
- Add missing tests: 404 route, invalid JSON, operation order
- Deduplicate ESLint config with sharedRules + nodeSourceRules
- Delete package-lock.json, add to .gitignore
- Remove ADR-001 (scope creep — separate PR)
- Acknowledge signPayload duplication with comment
- Fix README install instructions and placeholder values
- Improve changelog entry, tighten features bullet
- Add best-practices and API doc links to Building Your Own section

commit.message:
feat: 统一 AI 网关与新闻覆盖范围优化

## 第一块：统一 AI 网关与新闻覆盖范围

### 核心变更
- 新增统一 AI 网关（app/ai/gateway.py）：提供 execute(AIRequest) -> AIResponse 接口
- 新增新闻覆盖范围模块（app/news/coverage.py）：支持 daily_candidates / realtime_watchlist / all_market 多选并集
- 新闻情感分析改走统一网关：修复原先 system_prompt 参数不兼容问题
- AI 不可用或批次失败时保留原文不打分（sentiment_score: None），不再伪造"0/中性"

### 盘后链路集成
- 新闻舆情步骤从"固定前 100 只股票"改为"策略执行后按覆盖并集抓取"
- 支持传入 candidate_codes 参数，优先使用当日策略结果
- 配置项：NEWS_COVERAGE_SCOPES=["daily_candidates"]（可多选）

### API 增强
- GET /news/sentiment-trend/{ts_code} 返回覆盖状态 + 趋势数据
- 5 种状态：uncovered / covered_no_news / covered_pending_analysis / covered_neutral / covered_signal
- 明确区分"未覆盖"、"已覆盖但无新闻"、"已覆盖但未分析"等场景

### 前端适配
- 新增 SentimentCoverageInfo 类型，包含 5 种状态
- 趋势图下方显示覆盖状态提示（Alert 组件）
- 不再一律显示"暂无数据"，而是根据状态显示不同提示

## 第二块：彻底移除 Gemini 实现

### 删除文件
- app/ai/clients/gemini.py
- tests/unit/test_gemini_client.py

### 依赖清理
- pyproject.toml：移除 google-genai 和 google-auth
- uv.lock：重新生成锁文件

### 配置收口
- .env.example：AI_PROVIDER=codex（固定，为空则禁用）
- docker/.env.docker：同步更新
- README.md / CLAUDE.md：文档说明 Codex-only

### AI 网关强制校验
- AIGateway.__init__：非 codex 的 provider 会记录警告并禁用
- AIManager 改为委托 AIGateway，不再直接实例化客户端

### 测试适配
- 所有 AI 相关测试改为 Codex 口径
- 新增 test_ai_gateway.py（3 个测试）
- 新增 test_news_coverage.py（1 个测试）
- 更新 test_news_analyzer.py（9 个测试）

## 测试结果
- tests/unit/test_ai_gateway.py: 3 passed
- tests/unit/test_news_coverage.py: 1 passed
- tests/unit/test_news_analyzer.py: 9 passed
- tests/unit/test_ai_manager.py: 8 passed
- tests/unit/test_news_api.py: 8 passed
- web/npm run build: ✓ (3.65s)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
v1.6.4+5: data persistence, SOCMINT filters, custom sound picker

- OSINT/Events: persistent local cache from conflict start (600-day retention)
- Events: expanded source detection (X/Instagram/MOFA) + region detection
- Events: expanded conflict keywords (UAE/GCC/Arabic terms)
- SOCMINT: replaced severity filters with source-type (MISSILES/MOI/MOD/MOFA/GOV)
- OSINT: updated source categories (MILITARY/GOV/WIRE/NEWS/PRESS/TRACKING)
- Events source filters: added MOFA, X, IG alongside MOI/MOD/NCEMA/CENTCOM
- Settings: interactive sound picker per severity (preview + select)
- AlertSoundService: reads user preferences for custom sounds
- Refresh no longer clears data (preserves cached items)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement Milestone 8.3 — Session lifecycle and persistence

Session metrics tracking (XP, items, quests, locations) accumulated
by mutation tools. Rich LLM-generated session summaries via Claude
Haiku with structured key_events/decisions/next_hooks, falling back
to naive approach on failure. Agent-side reconnection handling with
2-minute grace period, background process pause/resume, and
welcome-back greeting. Narrative session ending via end_session tool
triggered by player goodbye phrases. Graceful LLM error handling
with retry + in-character fallback. Mid-stream failure guard prevents
garbled output on partial yield. Client reconnect timeout aligned to
2 minutes.
